front: Work: trilogy divided into Bubbles, Globes, and Foam, analyzing relations of space; partly intended as a follow-up to Heidegger's Being and Time
Philosopher: theorized three 'immune systems' — biological, societal, and psychological — through which humans interact with an inhospitable world
Work: defines cynicism as 'enlightened false consciousness' and distinguishes 'cheeky' Kynicism from modern Cynicism; widely read as response to 1960s-70s postmodern thought
Work: documents the history of 'anthropotechnics' and argues that 19th-century de-spiritualization led to the rise of the modern Olympics; title drawn from Rilke
back: You Must Change Your Life (Peter Sloterdijk)
work: You Must Change Your Life and Anthropotechnics

front: Philosopher: controversial advocacy for genetic technologies resulted in Jürgen Habermas calling this thinker a Nazi
Philosopher: deeply influenced Bruno Latour; wrote Thinker on Stage: Nietzsche's Materialism, attacking the notion of literary 'debts'
